info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Transaction Management API
info.description: The Transaction Processing API enables businesses to efficiently manage financial transactions within their applications. It allows users to initiate and process transactions securely while ensuring real-time data accuracy and integrity. This API is crucial for services that require transaction handling, such as e-commerce platforms, banking applications, and payment gateways, providing a reliable way to support business workflows. Developers can easily integrate this API to enhance user experiences and streamline financial operations.

post.description: This operation allows you to create a new transaction by providing the necessary details in the request body. Ensure all required fields are populated to successfully register the transaction.

requestBody.description: This request body is used to create a new transaction. It should contain the necessary details about the transaction being processed, such as amount, currency, and any associated metadata. Ensure that all required fields are included to avoid validation errors.

404.description: The specified resource could not be found.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

200.description: The request was processed successfully, and a response is returned.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createTransaction-Creat.title: Error Response Schema for Transactions POST Operation

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates the status of the operation. For error responses, this is typically false.

error.description: A brief description of the error.

message.description: Detailed error message providing more context.
message.example: Source account details not found for transaction processing.

path.description: The path of the request that resulted in the error.

trace.description: Stack trace of the error for debugging purposes.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for the user on how to handle the error.
suggestion.example: Verify account details and retry the transaction.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createTransaction-Creat.description: Schema representing the error response for the Transactions POST operation when a 404 status code is returned.

example.message: Source account details not found for transaction processing.

details.message: No active account exists for the provided account number.

example.suggestion: Verify account details and retry the transaction.

service.description: The name of the service where the error occurred.

errorCode.description: A specific code associated with the error.

message.description: A detailed message describing the specific error.

gateway.description: The gateway involved in the error, if applicable.

retryable.description: Indicates if the error can be retried.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createTransaction-Creat.title: ErrorResponse

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates if the request was successful or not.

error.description: A short description of the error.

message.description: A detailed message explaining the error.
message.example: Unable to process financial transaction due to internal service failure.

path.description: The path of the endpoint that caused the error.

trace.description: A trace ID to help identify the error in server logs.

details.description: Additional details about the specific error that occurred.

suggestion.description: A suggestion to the user on how to resolve the issue.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createTransaction-Creat.description: Represents the error response structure for the transactions operation when a 500 server error occurs.

example.message: Unable to process financial transaction due to internal service failure.

details.message: Payment gateway timeout while processing transaction request.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

customerId.description: The unique identifier for the customer initiating the transaction.

accountNumber.description: The account number from which the transaction is being made.

transactionType.description: The type of transaction (e.g., transfer, deposit, withdrawal).

amount.description: The amount of money involved in the transaction.

currency.description: The currency in which the transaction amount is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR).

beneficiaryAccount.description: The account number of the beneficiary in case of a transfer.

transactionDate.description: The date when the transaction is taking place, in ISO 8601 format.

paymentMode.description: The mode of payment (e.g., online, offline, card).

remarks.description: Any additional remarks or notes related to the transaction.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createTransaction-Creat.description: Request body schema for performing transactions, containing all necessary details.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createTransaction-Creat.title: Transaction Response

customerId.description: Identifier for the customer associated with the transaction.

accountNumber.description: The account number from which the transaction is made.

amount.description: The amount of money involved in the transaction.

currency.description: The currency in which the transaction amount is denominated.

status.description: Current status of the transaction (e.g., completed, pending).

processedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction was processed.

paymentMode.description: Mode of payment used for the transaction (e.g., card, bank transfer).

message.description: Optional message related to the transaction.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createTransaction-Creat.description: Schema representing the response for a successful transaction operation.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates the request headers that were used to select the response. Used for caching mechanisms.

Location.description: The URI of the newly created resource. This header provides the location of the resource created by the POST request.

content-type.description: The media type of the resource returned in the response. Indicates how to interpret the data.
